Title: An improved Nicotiana benthamiana bioproduction chassis provides novel insights into nicotine biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like (BBL) family; FAD-linked oxidoreductase-like
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steps common to nicotine, anabasine, and anatabine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream late step after pyridine–pyrrolidine coupling; oxidation/coupling-associated step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Unstable coupled nicotine-pathway intermediate derived from nicotinamide/nicotinic acid pyridine branch and pyrrolidine branch; exact substrate unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (S)-nicotine-forming oxidized intermediate / product leading to nicotine; exact immediate product unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a crucial late reaction in nicotine biosynthesis; evidence supports a role in stereoselective formation of (S)-nicotine and BBL-dependent loss of the C6 hydrogen from the pyridine ring, likely through oxidation of a coupled intermediate and possibly also stereoselective coupling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported for late nicotine biosynthesis in N. benthamiana; NbBBLa together with NbBBLb are the main functional BBL genes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or its immediate stereospecific precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense pyridine alkaloids; nicotine is biologically active and complicates purification in bioproduction chassis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves (nicotine/anabasine/anatabine measured); roots for DMN accumulation in mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by methyl jasmonate; expressed more strongly than NbBBLc and NbBBLd
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NbBBLa contributes to strong reduction of nicotine and related pyridine alkaloids when combined with NbBBLb knockout; BBL loss abolishes enantiomeric purity and causes racemic residual nicotine and DMN accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbBBLa is one of five N. benthamiana BBL genes and one of the two most highly expressed in roots. 2. Its expression is induced by MeJa. 3. Simultaneous inactivation with NbBBLb is sufficient to markedly lower leaf nicotine under induction. 4. Higher-order BBL knockouts reveal BBL dependence of stereoselective (S)-nicotine formation and specific C6 hydrogen loss. 5. Absence of functional BBLs causes accumulation of racemic nicotine and DMN.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling by LC-MS; chiral LC-MS; deuterated nicotinamide feeding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction; hydroponic seedling feeding with D4-nicotinamide
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNAs; CaMV 35S promoter for constitutive SpCas9 expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual nicotine remains due to likely spontaneous nonstereoselective chemistry; further pathway targeting may be required for stronger reduction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for generating low-nicotine N. benthamiana chassis for molecular farming; should be stacked at least with NbBBLb and potentially combined with other loci for deeper alkaloid reduction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact catalytic chemistry remains unresolved; authors propose BBL may catalyze oxidation of a coupled intermediate and possibly also stereoselective coupling. NbBBLa and NbBBLb are inferred to be the major active BBLs.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like (BBL) family; FAD-linked oxidoreductase-like
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steps common to nicotine, anabasine, and anatabine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream late step after pyridine–pyrrolidine coupling; oxidation/coupling-associated step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Unstable coupled nicotine-pathway intermediate; exact substrate unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (S)-nicotine-forming oxidized intermediate / product leading to nicotine; exact immediate product unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a crucial late reaction in nicotine biosynthesis; supports stereoselective production of natural (S)-nicotine and BBL-dependent C6 hydrogen loss, likely by oxidation of a coupled intermediate and possibly stereoselective coupling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; NbBBLb together with NbBBLa are the main functional BBL genes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or its immediate stereospecific precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense pyridine alkaloids; complicate purification in N. benthamiana bioproduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; roots for DMN in mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by methyl jasmonate; among the highest expressed NbBBLs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NbBBLb contributes to major reduction of nicotine and other pyridine alkaloids when combined with NbBBLa knockout; BBL loss causes racemic residual nicotine and DMN accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbBBLb is one of the two major expressed BBL genes in roots. 2. It is induced by MeJa. 3. Single mutants show no major phenotype, but combined inactivation with NbBBLa is sufficient to reduce nicotine substantially. 4. Higher-order BBL mutants show BBL-dependent stereoselectivity and isotope-specific hydrogen loss during nicotine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; LC-MS alkaloid profiling; chiral LC-MS; isotope feeding
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNAs; CaMV 35S promoter for constitutive SpCas9 expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Eliminating BBL function does not abolish all nicotine because spontaneous reactions still produce residual racemic nicotine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core engineering target for low-nicotine N. benthamiana chassis; especially effective when stacked with NbBBLa
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude NbBBLa and NbBBLb are the main NbBBL genes involved in pyridine alkaloid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like (BBL) family; FAD-linked oxidoreductase-like
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steps common to nicotine, anabasine, and anatabine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative BBL-catalyzed late step in nicotine biosynthesis similar to other NbBBLs, but with lower apparent contribution than NbBBLa/b
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as major control point; subordinate to NbBBLa/b
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots at lower levels than NbBBLa/b; induced by MeJa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additional knockout beyond NbBBLa/b does not significantly further lower nicotine levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbBBLc is a full-length BBL gene expressed in roots. 2. Expression increases after MeJa treatment. 3. Single mutant has no significant alkaloid phenotype. 4. Triple mutants including NbBBLc are not clearly lower in nicotine than NbBBLa/b double mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; LC-MS alkaloid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNAs; CaMV 35S promoter for SpCas9
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing this gene alone is insufficient to reduce nicotine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target with limited added value beyond NbBBLa/b knockout
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because directly tested in mutant combinations in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like (BBL) family; FAD-linked oxidoreductase-like
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steps common to nicotine, anabasine, and anatabine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative BBL-catalyzed late step in nicotine biosynthesis with minor contribution under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower expression in roots; not clearly induced by MeJa
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression; not induced by MeJa in reported assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single knockout has no significant effect on nicotine levels; higher-order inclusion does not further reduce alkaloids beyond major NbBBLa/b effects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbBBLd is a full-length BBL gene with low root expression. 2. Unlike NbBBLa-c, it was not induced by MeJa. 3. Single mutant showed no significant alkaloid phenotype. 4. Its additional inactivation in higher-order mutants did not significantly enhance nicotine reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; LC-MS alkaloid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNAs; CaMV 35S promoter for SpCas9
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low-priority auxiliary target relative to NbBBLa/b
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in quintuple mutant analysis but not a major determinant under tested conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: truncated berberine bridge enzyme-like protein D prime
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like (BBL) family; truncated/pseudogene-like due to premature stop codon
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steps common to pyridine alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely nonfunctional BBL homolog containing a premature stop codon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included in quintuple knockout background; no evidence that it independently contributes because it contains a premature stop codon
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbBBLd' was identified as a fifth BBL-like gene. 2. It contains a premature stop codon. 3. It was included in quintuple gene-editing designs, but the paper provides no evidence of an independent functional contribution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome assembly and sequence analysis; CRISPR genotyping in quintuple line
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNAs; CaMV 35S promoter for SpCas9
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely limited value as target owing to probable nonfunctional status
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although included experimentally, it is likely a nonfunctional paralog because of the premature stop codon.
